description_long: This object is a vertical black post supporting two distinct traffic signs. The lower sign is a classic red octagonal STOP sign with white lettering, easily recognizable and used to instruct vehicles to halt. Above it, a green rectangular street name sign is mounted perpendicular to the post, displaying white text for street identification. The post appears sturdy, likely made of metal, and is anchored at the base with a blocky support. The signs are affixed securely to the post, with the STOP sign facing directly forward and the street sign extending out to the side. The overall design is utilitarian, intended for clear visibility and durability in outdoor urban environments.
description: A black metal post with a red STOP sign and a green street name sign mounted above, designed for roadside visibility and durability.
description_view_0: Side view showing the thickness of the post and the edge of the STOP sign, with the green street sign extending outward.
description_view_1: Opposite side view, primarily showing the post and the edge of the green street sign, with minimal visibility of the STOP sign.
description_view_2: Front view displaying the full face of the red STOP sign and the green street sign above, both clearly legible.
description_view_3: Side view similar to view 1, showing the post and the green street sign extending outward, with the STOP sign edge visible.
